name: The Payne County Bank
description_short: Community bank serving Perkins, Oklahoma since 1898 with 125+ years of local ownership. Offers personal/business checking, savings, loans, mortgages, and digital banking services.
description_long:
Payne County Bank was incorporated January 3, 1898, nine years before Oklahoma achieved statehood. The institution has maintained continuous local ownership and management throughout its 125+ year history, including a significant 1993 transition to an Employee Stock Ownership Plan (ESOP) that ensured the bank would remain independently owned by its employees and officers. This commitment to community banking has made PCB a cornerstone institution in Perkins and surrounding areas.

The bank offers a comprehensive range of personal banking products including checking and savings accounts, Certificates of Deposit (CDs), Individual Retirement Accounts (IRAs), and credit cards. On the lending side, PCB provides personal loans, auto loans, home construction loans, mortgage loans, and mobile home loans. Additional services include safe deposit boxes, internet and mobile banking platforms, bill pay, wire transfer services, and mobile deposit capture technology that allows customers to deposit checks via smartphone.

PCB distinguishes itself through its multi-generational local leadership and ESOP structure, which aligns employee and community interests. The bank celebrates a documented history of service dating to 1898, including a major 1998 centennial remodeling and 2,500-square-foot expansion. Modern conveniences like mobile deposit and online banking coexist with traditional relationship-based banking practices. The institution also supports community through programs like Booster Bucks/Scholarship initiatives.

As a community bank with a single primary location in Perkins (plus a Chandler branch), Payne County Bank serves a defined geographic area and may have limited accessibility for customers outside Oklahoma. While the ESOP structure and 125-year history demonstrate stability, the bank's size and regional focus contrast with larger national institutions offering wider product ranges and geographic reach. Prospective customers should confirm current service availability and product terms directly, as website content does not specify loan rates, deposit insurance coverage details, or complete account fee structures. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Online and mobile banking with bill pay and wire transfers
pros: 125+ years of continuous operation with documented history of stable local leadership since 1898
Employee Stock Ownership Plan (ESOP) structure since 1993 ensures local ownership and community alignment
Modern digital services including mobile deposit capture, mobile app, and online banking
Comprehensive lending products: personal loans, auto loans, mortgages, home construction, and mobile home loans
Community-focused programs including Booster Bucks and scholarship offerings
Safe deposit boxes and full suite of payment services (bill pay, wire transfers)
Relationship-based banking approach with ability to contact loan officers directly
cons: Limited geographic presence restricted primarily to Perkins and Chandler, Oklahoma area
Website does not disclose current interest rates, APRs, or detailed fee structures for accounts and products
No information about FDIC insurance limits, deposit insurance coverage, or regulatory compliance details
Single primary location may create accessibility challenges for customers outside immediate service area
Website content incomplete (about page cuts off mid-sentence: 'Today, PCB')
best_for: Residents and businesses in Perkins and surrounding Payne County communities seeking local banking relationships
Customers prioritizing community bank values and employee ownership over national brand convenience
Small business owners and agricultural customers in central Oklahoma needing personalized lending services
Long-term community members seeking stable, locally-managed financial institution
diagnosis: Payne County Bank is best for residents of Perkins and surrounding Oklahoma communities who value long-term local ownership, relationship banking, and community ties over convenience of national branch networks. The primary caveat is geographic limitation—this is a regional community bank serving a defined area, not a statewide or national institution, making it inaccessible for customers outside central Oklahoma.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
